skill.name: dnanexus-integration
skill.description: DNAnexus cloud genomics platform. Build apps/applets, manage data (upload/download), dxpy Python SDK, run workflows, process FASTQ/BAM/VCF, for developing and executing genomics pipelines.
skill.summary: Cloud genomics platform integration for building apps, managing genomic data, and executing bioinformatics pipelines via the DNAnexus API and dxpy Python SDK.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 472 static analysis findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill contains only markdown documentation and reference files for the DNAnexus cloud genomics platform. Shell command backtick patterns appear inside markdown code blocks as documentation examples, not as executable scripts. The 'Windows SAM database' and 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are pattern-matching errors on API authentication environment variable examples and audit result JSON hashes. The critical heuristic combination finding (code execution + network + credential access) is dismissed because all patterns are legitimate documentation: code examples in markdown, URLs to official dnanexus.com resources, and a standard DX_SECURITY_CONTEXT env var example. No malicious intent, credential exfiltration, or unauthorized network behavior was found.

content.user_title: Run Genomics Workflows on DNAnexus Cloud
content.value_statement: Genomics researchers need a systematic way to build apps, manage sequencing data, and run bioinformatics pipelines on the DNAnexus cloud platform. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for using dxpy SDK, managing FASTQ/BAM/VCF files, executing workflows, and configuring app deployments.
actual_capabilities: Create and deploy DNAnexus Apps and Applets using dx-app-wizard and dx build
Upload, download, search, and organize genomic data files and records on the platform
Run analysis jobs, monitor execution status, and orchestrate multi-step workflows
Write Python scripts using the dxpy SDK for programmatic platform access
Configure dxapp.json with input/output specs, dependencies, and Docker containers
Process common bioinformatics file formats including FASTQ, BAM, and VCF
limitations: Requires an active DNAnexus account with project access and authentication token
Does not include pre-built bioinformatics tools or analysis algorithms
Cloud compute costs apply based on instance types and execution time used
Cannot run DNAnexus CLI commands without local dx-toolkit installation

use_cases.title: Upload and Analyze Sequencing Data
use_cases.description: Bioinformatics researchers can upload FASTQ files to a DNAnexus project, run alignment and variant calling applets, and download results for downstream analysis.

use_cases.title: Build and Deploy Analysis Pipelines
use_cases.description: Pipeline developers can create reproducible multi-step workflows with DNAnexus apps, define input/output specifications, and manage dependencies with Docker or Assets.

use_cases.title: Automate Genomic Data Management
use_cases.description: Data scientists can write dxpy scripts to batch upload experiment results, search for files by metadata properties, and organize project folders systematically.

prompt_templates.title: Upload a File to DNAnexus
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload the file sample.fastq to the project project-xxxx using the dxpy SDK. Include metadata properties for experiment name and sample ID.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs to transfer local sequencing data to a DNAnexus project for cloud-based analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Run an Applet and Download Results
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the applet applet-xxxx with input file file-xxxx on DNAnexus. Wait for the job to complete and download the output BAM file to the local machine.
prompt_templates.scenario: A researcher needs to execute a genomics analysis applet and retrieve the output for local review.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Multi-Step Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a DNAnexus workflow that chains together three applets: quality control, read alignment, and variant calling. Pass the output of each step as input to the next.
prompt_templates.scenario: A pipeline developer needs to construct a reproducible multi-step bioinformatics pipeline.

prompt_templates.title: Configure an App with Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a dxapp.json configuration for a BWA alignment app that specifies Python3 interpreter, samtools as a system dependency, Ubuntu 24.04 release, and input specs for FASTQ files and reference genome.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer needs to define the metadata, dependencies, and runtime environment for a new DNAnexus app.

output_examples.input: Upload a FASTQ file and run quality control filtering on DNAnexus.
output: Uploaded sample.fastq to project project-xxxx (file ID: file-abc123)
Started QC applet applet-qc-001 on uploaded file
Filtered output saved as filtered.fastq (file ID: file-def456)

output_examples.input: Search for all BAM files from experiment exp001 in the research project.
output: Found 12 BAM files matching experiment exp001
Files range in size from 2.1 GB to 8.4 GB
Each file has metadata: experiment, sample_id, reference_genome, alignment_date

output_examples.input: Create a new DNAnexus app that performs read trimming on input FASTQ files.
output: Configured dxapp.json with input spec for FASTQ files and quality threshold parameter
Wrote Python entry point with dxpy decorators for input/output handling
Built app with dx build --app (build ID: build-123)
Tested app successfully on sample dataset (5 MB input, 30 sec execution time)

best_practices: Use dxpy entry point decorators for Python app input/output handling and always wrap API calls in try-except blocks for robust error handling.
Organize data with consistent folder structures and metadata properties (experiment, sample, analysis date) to enable efficient searching and discovery.
Choose appropriate instance types for workloads and archive old data to reduce cloud compute and storage costs.
anti_patterns: Hardcoding authentication tokens or project IDs in source code instead of using environment variables or dx config.
Running large batch jobs sequentially instead of using parallel job submission with dxpy for multiple independent samples.
Mixing development applets and production apps in the same project without proper version control and testing.

faq.question: What is DNAnexus?
faq.answer: DNAnexus is a cloud platform for biomedical data analysis and genomics. It allows users to build apps, manage data, run workflows, and process bioinformatics file formats using the dxpy Python SDK.

faq.question: Do I need a DNAnexus account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need an active DNAnexus account with access to at least one project. You also need to install the dx-toolkit and authenticate using dx login or a security context token.

faq.question: What file formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill covers common bioinformatics formats including FASTQ (sequencing reads), BAM (aligned reads), VCF (variants), and general file types supported by the DNAnexus platform.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with the DNAnexus API?
faq.answer: You can authenticate by running dx login in the terminal, or by setting the DX_SECURITY_CONTEXT environment variable with your authentication token. Tokens are generated from the DNAnexus platform settings.

faq.question: Can I run this skill on local files without cloud access?
faq.answer: No. This skill requires DNAnexus API access. Local file processing is handled by the DNAnexus platform after upload. The skill provides guidance for the upload-analyze-download workflow.

faq.question: What programming languages does DNAnexus support for apps?
faq.answer: DNAnexus supports Python and Bash for app development. Python apps use the dxpy SDK with entry point decorators. Bash apps can use the dx command-line tool for platform operations.
